title: Machine Learning
author: Wooden_Roll_3583
selftext:
Built from scratch using Python, 8 neural network architectures, and a fully integrated live backend.



Here's what's inside:



📊 Live AI Signal Scanner — real-time BUY/SELL/HOLD signals across equities, crypto, forex & commodities with confidence scores up to 84%



🧠 Market Regime Detection — classifies current market into 5 regimes (Low Vol Bull, Crash/Panic, etc.) with probability distributions



🔬 Advanced Backtesting Engine — walk-forward validation, purged K-fold cross-validation, Monte Carlo simulation (1,000 runs), and robustness scoring



⚠️ Model Drift Detection — PSI + KS Statistic monitoring at both model and feature level to catch when models go stale



📈 Live AI Analysis — per-asset deep dives with 5-day price trajectory forecasts, risk/reward ratios, position sizing, and scenario analysis



🌐 Alternative Data Layer — macro indicators (Fed Funds Rate, CPI, VIX) combined with news, social, and institutional sentiment scores



🖥️ System Monitoring — 7/7 services live including Firestore, Stripe, FMP API, GCS Storage, and Email



This platform is live at [tradepro.streamlit.app](http://tradepro.streamlit.app) and currently serving real users with 99.9% uptime.



Built as a self-taught AI/ML Engineer — this project represents the convergence of quantitative finance, deep learning, and production engineering.



\#MachineLearning #AI #QuantitativeFinance #TradingTechnology #DataScience #Python #DeepLearning #FinTech #NeuralNetworks #OpenToWork

comments.author: nian2326076
comments.body:
If you're getting ready for a machine learning interview, make sure you know both the theory and how to apply it. Be able to talk about the architectures you've worked on and explain how parts of your projects function, like the live AI signal scanner or market regime detection. Know why you picked certain methods, like walk-forward validation or Monte Carlo simulations, over others. Be prepared to discuss how you deal with model drift and what PSI and KS statistics mean in that context.

Also, practice explaining your thought process and decisions on projects, as interviewers like to see how you tackle problems. If you need more resources, I've found [PracHub](https://prachub.com/?utm_source=reddit&utm_campaign=andy) useful for practical interview prep. Good luck!

comments.author: InnerGarage4519
comments.body: This looks totally Replit, and if you show this to anyone in the industry they will laugh at you lmao
